observed_evidence.real_entry_surface: Attempted the declared real checker surface `python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py adapter_error_path_hardening` (the verb that runs run_adapter_error_path_hardening / _assert_adapter_error_frontier_report_root_admission) — BLOCKED: python3 execution requires sandbox approval not grantable in this non-interactive run (same constraint the prior QA reported). As an alternate admitted static-evidence reader surface (acceptance #3 'distinguish states from admitted evidence/static files'), I read the recorded Frontier event project/.../adapter-timeout-frontier-lifecycle-hardening-0625/evidence/spine/events/0024-Frontier.json (frontier_kind='link_paused', adapter_error_records=0) and the live raw/report-thread.jsonl + raw/report-delivery.jsonl, which produced the real report_id values used in the reproduction above.
observed_evidence.symptom_reproduction: Independently reproduced the report-thread identity substring collision from this node's own task source + live incoming evidence (not the builder's fixture). Read the REAL emitted report-thread.jsonl: rows are report_slack_thread_parent_observation with NO 'building_id' field, only report_id='brick-protocol-adapter-timeout-frontier-lifecycle-hardening-0625-reroute1-building-started-event-2026-06-25T02-57-41-920648-00-00' (vessel form <project>-<building_id>-<kind>-event-<ts>). Hand-traced support/recording/adapter_error_frontier.py::_declaration_chain_report_id_matches_building (lines 622-645) with expected_building_id='adapter-timeout-frontier-lifecycle-hardening-0625' (the BASE building, a strict dash-prefix of the reroute id): text does NOT start with 'report:' so it takes the branch `text.startswith(f'{expected}-') or f'-{expected}-' in text`. The base building id is a dash-delimited prefix of the reroute id, so the substring '-adapter-timeout-frontier-lifecycle-hardening-0625-' (closing dash is the one before 'reroute1') IS present in the reroute1 report_id => returns True. A reroute1 report-thread row is therefore admitted as belonging to the base building. The boundary-delimiter fix only made the legacy 'report:<id>:<kind>' branch exact; the live vessel/legacy dash form still collides on prefix-related building ids.

regression_risks: Acceptance #5 (reject substring-collision building ids): the boundary fix only hardened the 'report:' branch (exact source_id==expected). The live vessel/legacy dash form path `text.startswith(f'{expected}-') or f'-{expected}-' in text` (adapter_error_frontier.py:645) still admits a report row from a DIFFERENT building whose id is a dash-prefix or dash-infix of expected. Demonstrated with this very family: base id 'adapter-timeout-frontier-lifecycle-hardening-0625' collides with reroute id '...-0625-reroute1'. _root_holds_only_declaration_chain_artifacts therefore cannot distinguish base vs reroute report rows when the row lacks a building_id field (the live rows do lack it).

Acceptance #2 'Preserve unrelated evidence': preservation/marker only happens when `not overwrite_existing` (line 192). On the resume path (which must pass overwrite_existing=True because the root already exists), a partial_write_risk root takes effective_overwrite_existing=True (lines 240-243) and write_building_lifecycle overwrites root files (building-work.json, raw-manifest.json, evidence-manifest.json, capture/events.jsonl) with no preservation and no partial-write-risk marker.

Acceptance #3 'root exists without frontier' distinguishable as a static file: _adapter_error_existing_root_state returns 'root_exists_without_frontier' (empty dir) and 'not_directory' as local strings that nothing consumes — no marker is emitted for them. Only 'partial_write_risk' yields a static marker. For an empty pre-existing root with overwrite_existing=False, control falls through to capture.py which raises FileExistsError with no frontier (the original concern #1 symptom persists narrowly for the empty-root trigger).

Acceptance #4 predicate alignment: divergence is only DOCUMENTED one-directionally (docstring at adapter_error_frontier.py:501-509). The walker preflight (walker_kernel.py:2411, narrow _root_holds_only_intake_plan_artifact) runs before the adapter and rejects any declaration-chain root with overwrite=False, and on resume overwrite=True bypasses preservation — so the widened frontier predicate + partial-write-risk marker are effectively bypassed on the walker path in both overwrite modes. run.py/walker_kernel.py carry no reciprocal note pointing to the wider frontier predicate; no shared constant anchors them, so future drift is unguarded from the preflight side.
